```
diff --git a/src/vfs.rs b/src/vfs.rs
index d60a3ad..da6566b 100644
--- a/src/vfs.rs
+++ b/src/vfs.rs
@@ -82,6 +82,56 @@ impl Vfs {
             format!("/{}", result)
         }
     }
+
+    /// Create directory at path (mkdir_all style). Creates any missing parent directories.
+    /// Returns Err(()) if any path component exists and is not a directory.
+    pub fn mkdir(&amp;mut self, path: &amp;str) -&gt; Result&lt;(), ()&gt; {
+        let abs = self.resolve_to_absolute(path);
+        let segments: Vec&lt;&amp;str&gt; = abs.split('/').filter(|s| !s.is_empty()).collect();
+        if segments.is_empty() {
+            return Ok(());
+        }
+        let mut indices: Vec&lt;usize&gt; = vec![];
+        for segment in segments {
+            let current = Self::get_mut_at(&amp;mut self.root, &amp;indices);
+            match current {
+                Node::Dir { children, .. } =&gt; {
+                    let pos = children.iter().position(|c| c.name() == segment);
+                    match pos {
+                        Some(i) =&gt; {
+                            if !children[i].is_dir() {
+                                return Err(());
+                            }
+                            indices.push(i);
+                        }
+                        None =&gt; {
+                            children.push(Node::Dir {
+                                name: segment.to_string(),
+                                children: vec![],
+                            });
+                            indices.push(children.len() - 1);
+                        }
+                    }
+                }
+                Node::File { .. } =&gt; return Err(()),
+            }
+        }
+        Ok(())
+    }
+
+    /// Mutable reference to node at path of child indices from the given node.
+    fn get_mut_at&lt;'a&gt;(node: &amp;'a mut Node, path: &amp;[usize]) -&gt; &amp;'a mut Node {
+        if path.is_empty() {
+            return node;
+        }
+        match node {
+            Node::Dir { children, .. } =&gt; {
+                let i = path[0];
+                Self::get_mut_at(&amp;mut children[i], &amp;path[1..])
+            }
+            Node::File { .. } =&gt; unreachable!("path must follow dirs only"),
+        }
+    }
 }
 
 /// Normalize a path to Unix style: backslash -&gt; slash, strip Windows drive,
diff --git a/tests/vfs_tests.rs b/tests/vfs_tests.rs
index 8615809..962321d 100644
--- a/tests/vfs_tests.rs
+++ b/tests/vfs_tests.rs
@@ -24,3 +24,11 @@ fn resolve_absolute_path_missing_returns_err() {
     let vfs = Vfs::new();
     assert!(vfs.resolve_absolute("/foo").is_err());
 }
+
+#[test]
+fn mkdir_creates_path() {
+    let mut vfs = Vfs::new();
+    vfs.mkdir("/foo/bar").unwrap();
+    let n = vfs.resolve_absolute("/foo/bar").unwrap();
+    assert!(n.is_dir());
+}
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I have the diff and the full vfs.rs. Let me run the test to confirm it passes, then summarize.

Observations from the diff:

**mkdir implementation:**
- Uses `resolve_to_absolute(path)` so relative paths work (mkdir_all style).
- Splits absolute path into segments, skips empty (so "/" or "" → empty segments → Ok(()) for root).
- Walks segments; for each segment gets current dir via `get_mut_at`, then either finds existing child (must be Dir or Err) or creates new Dir and appends index.
- Correctly creates parent dirs (mkdir_all style).
- Returns Err(()) when a path component exists and is not a directory (file in the way).

**get_mut_at:**
- Recursive helper that follows indices into the tree. For File hits unreachable! (path should only traverse dirs). Looks correct.

**Test:**
- mkdir_creates_path: creates "/foo/bar", then resolve_absolute("/foo/bar") and asserts is_dir(). This checks that the path was created and is a directory. Good.

**Potential issues:**
1. **Critical?** — If path is "/" or resolves to "/", segments is empty and we return Ok(()). That's correct (root already exists).
2. **Path normalization:** resolve_to_absolute uses normalize_path, so "foo/bar", "./foo/bar", "/foo/bar" all work. Good.
3. **Empty segment in the middle?** normalize_path can produce "foo//bar" → segments would be ["foo","bar"] since we filter empty. So we're good.
4. **get_mut_at on File:** The only way we get to Node::File in get_mut_at is if a previous segment was a file and we pushed its index. But in mkdir we only push indices when we're at a Dir and either find a Dir child or create one. So we never push an index to a File. So the unreachable! is correct. Actually wait - we push indices when we find an existing Dir (indices.push(i)) or when we create a new Dir (indices.push(children.len() - 1)). So we only ever traverse into Dir nodes. The File branch in get_mut_at would only be hit if somehow path contained an index pointing to a File. So we're good.
5. **Error type:** Returns Result<(), ()> - same as resolve_absolute. Fine.
6. **Test coverage:** Only one test: mkdir_creates_path for "/foo/bar". No test for: mkdir("/") or mkdir("."), mkdir when parent is a file (should Err), mkdir on existing dir (should Ok), relative path like "foo/bar". So coverage could be broader but the task said "test passes" and "mkdir creates path, mkdir_all style" - the single test does show creation and implicitly mkdir_all (creates /foo and /foo/bar). So acceptable.

Let me run the test.
